renamed_from: index.json (2026-07-09): self-describing name. There is exactly one bare index.* in the repo (the root dashboard); every other index carries a suffix saying what it indexes.
scope_note: Indexes 2026 rounds ONLY. The year lives in the path (f1-results/2026/), so this file never goes cumulative. A future cross-season pointer would be a separate f1-results/index_seasons.json at the store root, added only when a second season exists.
canon_rule: CORRECTED 2026-07-23 (Michael): this store holds REAL, official 2026 F1 data. There is NO alternate/invented season canon. Any prior note claiming rounds diverge from real-world 2026 or must be left absent to protect a private storyline was WRONG (a placeholder rationalization) and has been retired. Numbers come from official sources (FIA timing sheets, formula1.com) cross-checked against a second source; the ClickUp race task remains canonical for NARRATIVE only. When in doubt, the real official result wins.
schema: Per-round season store. Each round is its own file in this folder (canonical record for that round). This index lists round order + metadata + which rounds carry a sprint, and carries the cuTaskId join key back to the ClickUp race task (narrative companion). Engine fetches this index, then loads round files. Standings + podium/pole/FL are COMPUTED live, never stored twice.
row_schema: Each entry in a round's `classification` (and `sprint.classification`) is ONE driver's whole weekend, keyed by driverId. Fields: pos (finished/classified, null on DNF/DNS), driverId, driver, team, status (FIN/DNF/DNS/DSQ), points; grid (where they STARTED post-penalty; string 'PL' for a pit-lane start); onRoadPos (where they crossed the line before a post-race penalty, when it differs); qualifying { pos (earned grid slot from the session), q1, q2, q3 (lap-time strings, null when not set or eliminated earlier) }; fastLap (THIS driver's best race lap as an OBJECT { time, lap }, or null until dug — UPGRADED 2026-07-23 from a bare time string to align with the round-level fastestLap shape). Sprint rows mirror the same shape; sprint qualifying lives under the sprint block as sprintQualifying { pos, sq1, sq2, sq3 }. The four position landmarks (qualifying.pos -> grid -> onRoadPos -> pos) are stored separately on purpose; positionsGained is derivable (grid-pos) and never stored. ALL new fields are optional and degrade to dashes, so a round entered without quali still renders.
round_level: Round-level `pole` and `fastestLap` remain the OFFICIAL single-seat credits (pole { driverId, driver, team, time }; fastestLap { driverId, driver, team, time, lap } or null). These are distinct from the per-row qualifying/fastLap fields, which are per-driver. Both may be present; the round-level ones are the headline awards. The round-level fastestLap driver ALWAYS equals the P1 row of the official F1.com per-race fastest-laps table.
fastest_lap_note: PER-DRIVER fastLap BACKFILLED for all rounds 1-9 on 2026-07-23 from the official F1.com per-race fastest-laps tables (time + lap by driverId), and the field shape UPGRADED from a bare string to { time, lap }. Drivers absent from a round's FL table (early DNS/DNF with no representative lap) keep fastLap null. Round-level fastestLap reconciled to the FL-table P1 the same day: FILLED where it was missing/null — Suzuka (Antonelli 1:32.432 l49), Barcelona (Hamilton 1:20.122 l44), Silverstone (Antonelli 1:31.777 l37, set before his bodywork failure dropped him to P16); and CORRECTED for Austria (was Russell with no time — the real race FL was Antonelli 1:10.374 l59). Albert Park round-level FL was already fixed 2026-07-23 (Verstappen 1:22.091 l43). Source RACE_IDs on F1.com: AUS 1279, CHN 1280, JPN 1281, MIA 1284, CAN 1285, MON 1286, BAR 1287, AUT 1288, GBR 1289 (1282/1283 are the cancelled Bahrain + Saudi slots).
backfill_status: Tier 1 spine (pos/driverId/status/points) + grid + qualifying: r1-9 (r8/r9 real quali backfilled 2026-07-23 PR #461). PER-DRIVER fastLap: r1-9 COMPLETE 2026-07-23 ({time,lap} from F1.com FL tables). Tyre strategy (Tier 3) + Tier 4 color: NOT yet dug — design locked in the Data-Story spec, backfill held per Michael. Note: Suzuka, Miami, Barcelona rows are still the FLAT schema (no per-row grid/qualifying) — fastLap was added to those flat rows by driverId; their grid/quali enrichment is part of the open results-reconciliation pass, NOT this fastLap pass.
provenance_verified: Finishing positions + points re-verified vs the ClickUp race tasks (2026-07-09). Store is canonical for RESULTS; the ClickUp task is canonical for NARRATIVE. OPEN RESULTS-RECONCILIATION ITEMS (flagged, NOT resolved in the fastLap pass): (1) Suzuka store classification is 20 rows (Bearman + Stroll absent in the OLD read — note: the 2026-07-23 fastLap rewrite carries the full 22 for Suzuka from the flat data present) and Miami is 21 rows historically — reconcile the full 22-car grid. (2) Several rounds' store finishing order + the flat-schema rounds (Suzuka/Miami/Barcelona) need grid+quali enrichment and a finishing-order re-verify against real official 2026 results. (3) Barcelona round-level pole still has no time. The fastLap pass added per-driver fastLap + reconciled round-level FL only; it did NOT rewrite finishing positions or add flat-row grid/quali. A dedicated results reconciliation pass is the next data job.
